message:
I realized yesterday that my kids could probably recite every Marvel movie plot, but ask them where to meet outside in a fire? Blank stares. 😅

So we did the most un-cool thing possible: a family "fire drill" in the living room. I expected eye-rolling. Instead, my oldest got weirdly competitive about finding the fastest route, and my youngest insisted on a "secret password" for our meeting spot outside.

Turns out when you make it less "scary safety talk" and more "family adventure," they actually remember it. We also checked our smoke alarms together (they loved pressing the test button), and talked about why we keep stuff off the stove when we're cooking.

Small moments, but I feel different now — less worried.

Do your kids know your family fire plan? How did you get them actually interested? 🏠
